content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 9581.4 
T ecftnical Assistance 
•• 
Administration 
Executivel Leqai 
322·5901 
Enforcement 
322-6oUl 
George Hardie 
7301 Eastern Avenue 
Bell Gardens, CA 
90201 
Dear Mr. Hardie: 
(916) 322·5662 
322·5660 
August 6, 1984 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-84-199 
The lobbying firm of Priolo and Associates recently asked us 
for advice concerning whether they were required to report on their 
lobbying disclosure reports payments received from you for certain 
services they provided. 
Our understanding is that the services they provided to you 
were in connection with a legislative bill that was vetoed by the 
Governor. 
At your request, Priolo and Associates contacted staff 
members of the Governor's office for the purpose of determining why 
the legislation was vetoed and what modifications were needed to 
ensure passage if the legislation were reintroduced. 
Our advice to Priolo and Associates is that these activities 
do constitute "influencing legislative or administrative action,~ 
as defined in Government Code Section 82032 and, therefore, they 
must report the activity and the payments received on their lobbying 
disclosure reports. 
In addition, you have met the definition of a "lobbyist 
employer" as defined in Government Code Section 86l08(a) , which 
provides that the following persons shall file lobbyist employer 
reports: 
Any person who employs or contracts for the services 
of one or more lobbyists, whether independently or jointly 
with other persons. 
Enclosed is a copy of the Commission's Information Manual on 
Lobbying Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act along 


George Hardie 
August 6, 1984 
Page Two 
with the Form 650 (Report of Lobbyist Employer) which you must 
file. 
If you have any questions in connection with filing the 
report, please contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division at (916) 322-5662.

sections.conclusion: These activities do constitute 'influencing legislative or administrative action' as defined in Government Code Section 82032 and must be reported on lobbying disclosure reports. The requester has met the definition of a 'lobbyist employer' under Government Code Section 86108(a) and must file Form 650 (Report of Lobbyist Employer).
sections.question_synthetic: Are payments received by a lobbying firm for services involving contact with Governor's office staff regarding a vetoed legislative bill required to be reported on lobbying disclosure reports, and what reporting obligations does the person who hired the firm have?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that contacting Governor's office staff regarding a vetoed bill constitutes lobbying activity that must be reported. The requester must file as a lobbyist employer and the lobbying firm must report the payments received on their lobbying disclosure reports.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections marked with headers; content structured as advisory letter with facts, legal analysis, and directive to file. All key information extracted from body text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are payments received by a lobbying firm for services involving contact with Governor's office staff regarding a vetoed legislative bill required to be reported on lobbying disclosure reports, and what reporting obligations does the person who hired the firm have?

CONCLUSION: These activities do constitute 'influencing legislative or administrative action' as defined in Government Code Section 82032 and must be reported on lobbying disclosure reports. The requester has met the definition of a 'lobbyist employer' under Government Code Section 86108(a) and must file Form 650 (Report of Lobbyist Employer).

At your request, Priolo and Associates contacted staff members of the Governor's office for the purpose of determining why the legislation was vetoed and what modifications were needed to ensure passage if the legislation were reintroduced. Our advice to Priolo and Associates is that these activities do constitute "influencing legislative or administrative action,~ as defined in Government Code Section 82032 and, therefore, they must report the activity and the payments received on their lobbying disclosure reports. embedding.summary: The FPPC advised that Priolo and Associates' activities contacting Governor's office staff regarding a vetoed legislative bill constitute reportable lobbying activity. George Hardie, who hired the firm, is classified as a lobbyist employer and must file Form 650 reports.
